signature.morphology_notes: Migratory precursor cells in SGZ; proliferative (Ki67+); DCX+ (type-3) or Nestin+/DCX+ (type-2b); last mitotic stage before postmitotic differentiation

marker_overlap.note: No classical protein markers detected in atlas transcriptomic signature. Atlas markers: Eomes (=Tbr2, type-2b TF), Sox6, Neurod1, Meis1/2, Igfbpl1, Lmo1, Slc17a6, Celsr1, Tenm1.

candidates.rationale: Strongest candidate: correct anatomical location (SGZ), correct NT type (Glut), curator-annotated as 'early neuroblast' with type-2b marker Eomes/Tbr2. Marker overlap zero but likely methodological difference (protein vs. transcriptome). Curator mapping notes suggest Eomes+ signature confirms type-2b progenitor identity, with MERFISH validation of Eomes/Igfbpl1 at DG-STR boundary.

marker_overlap.note: No classical markers detected. Atlas markers: Vim (progenitor marker), Neurod4, Nhlh1, Eomes (=Tbr2), Nrn1, Zic5, Igfbpl1, Mfap4, Slc17a6.

matched_regions: UBERON:0001885 (DG proper) — parent region, not SGZ; anatomical assignment uncertain (noted 'CTX?' in original data)

candidates.rationale: Secondary candidate: curator-annotated 'early neuroblast' with type-2b markers (Vim, Eomes), but location uncertain and not specifically SGZ. Vim (vimentin) is consistent with type-2b progenitor identity. MERFISH validates Eomes and Igfbpl1. Worth investigating despite anatomical ambiguity.

marker_overlap.note: No classical markers. Atlas markers: Eomes, Calb2 (calretinin, distinct from Calbindin), Prox1, Bhlhe22, Draxin, Mndal, Adamts18, Igfbpl1.

matched_regions: UBERON:0001885 (DG proper, DGdo/me) — parent of SGZ; likely beyond neuroblast stage

candidates.rationale: Tertiary candidate: curator-annotated 'late neuroblast' with Eomes still expressed (type-3 stage marker) but also Calb2 and Prox1, indicating transition toward immature granule neuron. Location is mature GCL, not SGZ. More consistent with dg_immature_granule_neuron (next stage in lineage).

non_candidates_summary: 4 atlas nodes scored below threshold (0.25): clusters 0514, 0515 (immature granule neurons, wrong developmental stage), 0516, 0517 (piriform cortex and endopiriform area, distant brain regions). These represent later developmental stages or off-target cell types. Key finding: systematic absence of classical DCX, Ki67, PSA-NCAM, Nestin protein markers in atlas transcriptomic data is likely a methodological difference (protein immunohistochemistry vs. single-cell RNA-seq). Atlas nodes compensate with TF markers (Eomes/Tbr2, Prox1, Neurod1) and location information. Candidate neuroblast clusters (0511, 0512, 0513) share Eomes/Tbr2 signature with curator-validated type-2b identity; this TF signature may be a more reliable molecular correlate than classical protein markers alone.
